evidence.description: Abolhassan Banisadr was the first president of the Islamic Republic before being deposed in 1981. Both Pahlavi and Banisadr ended up in exile but represented fundamentally different visions — monarchist restoration versus republican opposition.

editorial_notes: Both are exiled opposition figures but from opposing traditions — Pahlavi (monarchist/secular democratic) vs Banisadr (republican/revolutionary). They represent competing claims to post-revolution legitimacy.
